notes: Official Origin product capture for TreasuryBench V1.
response:
As a UX consultant managing uneven income, reviewing your recurring charges is a proactive way to maintain the "low-admin" system you prefer. Your data indicates a significant portion of your overhead is tied to your professional software stack and healthcare.
Business recurrings (UX consulting)
Given your self-employed status, these charges represent your baseline business overhead. Since these expenses involve tax considerations for your Schedule C filing, I'll explain the concepts educationally. Per CFP Board standards, consult a qualified tax advisor for advice specific to your situation before making decisions.
WeWork Austin: $350.00 per month (Business rent)
Adobe Creative Cloud: $59.99 per month (Software)
Webflow: $39.00 per month (Software)
Amazon Web Services (AWS): $82.44 per month (Software)
Google Workspace: $14.40 per month (Software)
Figma Professional: $15.00 per month (Software)
Your combined monthly software overhead currently reaches $210.83. Since you use both Figma and Adobe, it may be worth evaluating if there is any overlap in your current project workflows that could allow for consolidation.
Personal and mixed recurrings
These items are your fixed monthly obligations for your household and personal health.
HealthCare.gov Marketplace: $415.00 per month (Healthcare)
Costco Wholesale: $238.62 per month (Groceries/Subscription)
Spectrum Internet: $79.99 per month (Internet)
Visible Wireless: $35.00 per month (Phone)
Annual Renewal Membership Fee: $27.08 per month (Calculated from your $325 annual charge)
Your Costco spending of $238.62 is currently flagged as a recurring monthly subscription in your data. If this was a one-time bulk purchase rather than a membership or recurring delivery, we can adjust your budget categories to reflect it as variable spending instead.
Opportunities for review
Based on your current patterns, your total identified recurring baseline is approximately $1,356.52 per month.
Software stack audit: You are paying for five distinct software platforms totaling $210.83 monthly. For a UX consultant, these are often essential, but reviewing the "Professional" vs. "Basic" tiers for Webflow or Figma could potentially save $20–$40 monthly.
Internet allocation: Since you work from a WeWork and maintain a home office, a portion of your $79.99 Spectrum bill may be eligible for a home office deduction.
Annual fees: Your $325 annual membership fee (which costs you $27.08 monthly) is a significant one-time hit to your cash flow. Setting aside $28 each month in your "tax reserve" or a separate bucket can prevent this from disrupting your personal spending when the next bill arrives.
Would you like to see how these recurring costs compare to your average freelance income over the last few months? 📈
